definition: Examine or explore a topic from multiple perspectives. Implies consideration of more than one viewpoint or dimension.

expected_response.format: Multiple perspectives or factors presented; may conclude with a synthesis or balanced summary
do: Present at least two perspectives or factors
do_not: Give opinion without evidence for each perspective

mark_expectation: Higher-order marks; requires breadth of understanding across multiple dimensions
question_stem_examples: Discuss the advantages and disadvantages of mechanical harvesting in quality-focused regions.
Discuss the role of terroir in explaining regional wine character.
mentor_hint: Identify 2-3 perspectives ('On one hand... On the other hand...'). Support each with evidence. End with synthesis: 'The relative importance depends on...'
